This field needs to both flow through as a Contact and Deal. We would need to make sure the lowest level sales partner in the hierarchy is who is who the Merchant is assigned to as both a HubSpot Contact and HubSpot Deal. As a reference point, sometimes we have referral partners who earn revenue from the merchant account. The lowest level referral partner would be the HubSpot Contact.
column8: Currently, we have the sales partner listed as the Contact Owner in HubSpot. We do not have a Sales Code associated anywhere. What is going to be the best practice for making sure the right sales partner is attributed to both the Contact and the Deal?

column7: If a merchant has multiple merchant accounts, we will need the fields to be updated with the newest MID starting first. For example, Newest MID 1 / 2nd New MID 2 / Original MID 3

1. On the 3rd of each month, the fees are debited from a merchant's bank account. Most merchant accounts have a Monthly Platform Fee that is billed whether a merchant has begun processing yet or not. If a merchant signs up towards the end of the month, often they are not actually live and processing and have their monthly fee debited, which moves the status in CoPilot from Boarded to Live, even though the merchant is not truly live and processing transactions yet. What about merchants who have not processed yet, but automatically have the CoPilot status changed to Live. 2. We need to make sure the current HubSpot WorkFlows occur where it sends an automated Email Template to the Merchant about Next Steps with CardChamp.

column8: Almost all multiple locations are linked for PCI (meaning one account covers the rest). In the rare instance, where a contact owner has more than one location and they are not linked and one is PCI Compliant and one is not PCI Compliant, can we have the account the is NOT PCI Compliant override this contact property?
